Forecasting the CO2 Time Series with Meta Prophet

Coursework 1

Shante Constable

Spring term 2025

Libraries

library(prophet)
## Loading required package: Rcpp
## Loading required package: rlang
library(zoo)
## 
## Attaching package: 'zoo'
## The following objects are masked from 'package:base':
## 
##     as.Date, as.Date.numeric

Introduction

This project uses the data in Meta Prohpet to study and forecast,in R, the built-in C02 time series.

Data

co2_dataframe = data.frame(
 ds = as.Date(as.yearmon(time(co2))),
 y = as.numeric(co2)
)

head(co2_dataframe)
##           ds      y
## 1 1959-01-01 315.42
## 2 1959-02-01 316.31
## 3 1959-03-01 316.50
## 4 1959-04-01 317.56
## 5 1959-05-01 318.13
## 6 1959-06-01 318.00

The co2 data is a time series, taken monthly so i converted it into ds and y becasue of Prophet. ## Prophet model

co2_prophet_model = prophet(co2_dataframe)
## Disabling weekly seasonality. Run prophet with weekly.seasonality=TRUE to override this.
## Disabling daily seasonality. Run prophet with daily.seasonality=TRUE to override this.
future_co2_dates = make_future_dataframe(co2_prophet_model, periods = 24, freq = "month")
co2_forecast = predict(co2_prophet_model, future_co2_dates)
plot(co2_prophet_model, co2_forecast)

Here I created a Prophet model, made dates for the future and predicted the next 24 months.

Comment

The forecast continues the overall upward trend in the time series. There is also a repeating seasonal pattern acroos the years. So Prophet has obtained both long-term and seasonal patterns.
